days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: First impressions and local culinary delights

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Your flight from Penang arrives. Clear customs and collect your luggage before heading to the Express Train to the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Drop off your bags at this stylish hotel conveniently located near Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station, known for its stunning harbor views.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Harbour
timeline.detail: Take a relaxing stroll along the promenade and enjoy stunning views of the skyline. Perfect for photos at sunset.
timeline.mapQuery: Victoria Harbour, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Savor Hong Kong's famous wonton noodles at this renowned establishment, a favorite among locals and tourists alike. Expect delicious handmade noodles and shrimp wontons.

timeline.title: Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Enjoy the stunning light and sound show set against the Hong Kong skyline, a perfect way to end your first day. Watch from the promenade for the best view.

timeline.title: Relax at the hotel
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel ICON and unwind or enjoy a late-night drink at their rooftop bar with breathtaking views.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card upon arrival for convenient travel on public transport.
Book iconic restaurants in advance to avoid long waits.
Visit museums in Tsim Sha Tsui for free or discounted entry on Wednesdays.
Download the MTR app for easy navigation and ticket information.
Try local street food at Temple Street Night Market if time permits.

days.title: Exploring the Cultural Side of Hong Kong
days.subtitle: History, food, and shopping

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a classic Hong Kong breakfast of scrambled eggs and toast at a beloved local eatery famous for its fast service.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Explore the rich history of Hong Kong through engaging exhibitions. Don't miss the section on Hong Kong's colonial past.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy Michelin-star dim sum at Tim Ho Wan, known for its pork buns and shrimp dumplings. Be prepared to queue but it's worth the wait.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Sham Shui Po, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shop at Ladies Market
timeline.detail: Browse through countless stalls selling clothes, accessories, and souvenirs. Don’t forget to haggle for the best price.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Tai Yang Shop for Dessert
timeline.detail: Wrap up your day with traditional Hong Kong sweets at Tai Yang Shop, the perfect place to try mango desserts and egg tarts.
timeline.mapQuery: Tai Yang Shop, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Rest
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel and take some time to relax and refresh before dinner.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Yung Kee
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious meal of roast goose at Yung Kee, a local institution famous for its Cantonese cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: Yung Kee Restaurant, Central, Hong Kong

highlights: Dim sum at Tim Ho Wan
localTips: Try to visit museums earlier in the day to avoid large crowds.
Use the MTR for the quickest way to get around the city.
Be adventurous and try local snacks sold from street vendors.
Bring cash as not all stalls accept card payments.
Download an offline map to navigate through busy markets easily.

days.title: Final Day in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Last-minute experiences and departure

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at the hotel, sampling various local and continental dishes in a relaxed setting.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car
timeline.detail: Experience breathtaking views of Lantau Island as you ride the cable car to Ngong Ping. Be sure to bring your camera!
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping 360, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Big Buddha
timeline.detail: Explore the statue of the Tian Tan Buddha, a majestic sight, which is a significant cultural landmark in Hong Kong.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Ngong Ping, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery
timeline.detail: Enjoy a vegetarian lunch served at the monastery's dining room, offering delicious local flavors that complement your experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery, Ngong Ping, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Pack
timeline.detail: Head back to the hotel to pack your belongings and check out, ensuring you have everything for your journey home.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Head to Airport
timeline.detail: Catch the Airport Express back to the airport in time for your departure flight. Make sure to arrive early for check-in.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

localTips: If time allows, stop by the Wisdom Path near the Big Buddha.
Consider purchasing items at the Ngong Ping market for souvenirs.
Confirm your flight details to ensure timely arrival at the airport.
Check the weather before heading to Ngong Ping; visibility affects views.
Ensure your Octopus card has enough balance for transport back to the airport.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Stylish hotel near Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station with excellent harbor views.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Style and comfort near the MTR.
For public transport and some shops.

budget.item: Meals and Drinks (average RM 70/day per person)

budget.note: Fast transfer from the airport to the city.

urgent.label: Book Ngong Ping 360 tickets in advance
urgent.note: Avoid queues and guarantee your spot.
